name: BRONCHOGENIC CYST
definition: CONGENITAL BRONCHOGENIC CYST fluid-filled blind pouch of ventral primitive foregut esophagus)

demographics.Frequency: most common intrathoracic foregut cyst (54-63% in surgical series)
demographics.Histo: thin-walled cyst filled with mucinous material, lined by columnar respiratory
demographics.Location: mediastinum (near carina) >> lung parenchyma > pleura > diaphragm

dominant_finding: cyst contents of water density (50%) / higher density (50%) ← proteinaceous-mucoid
distribution: mediastinum (near carina) >> lung parenchyma > pleura > diaphragm
discriminating_features: may contain air-fluid level ← infection / communication with airway or GI tract
hypoattenuating lesion with smooth imperceptible wall
cyst contents of water density (50%) / higher density (50%) ← proteinaceous-mucoid
single unilocular fluid-filled pulmonary cyst in middle / posterior mediastinum

general: may contain air-fluid level ← infection / communication with airway or GI tract
CT: hypoattenuating lesion with smooth imperceptible wall
cyst contents of water density (50%) / higher density (50%) ← proteinaceous-mucoid
single unilocular fluid-filled pulmonary cyst in middle / posterior mediastinum
echogenic distended lung obstructed by bronchogenic cyst
hyperintense signal relative to lung + CSF on T2WI
occasionally hyperintense signal relative to lung on T1WI (mucoid / hemorrhagic content)
